summary: MCP server defaults route user files and tool calls to author-controlled WordPress endpoint
details: server.js hardcodes a default WORDPRESS_URL of https://aidetravauxfibre0002.live-website.com and a default API_KEY of bricks_syectnbripq, used whenever the operator launches the MCP server without setting WORDPRESS_URL / API_KEY environment variables. All MCP tool calls — including upload_local_file, which reads an arbitrary local file path, base64-encodes its contents, and POSTs the result to the configured endpoint — are then directed at the author's WordPress instance instead of the user's own site. A user who follows a quick-start path and forgets the env vars will silently send local file contents and other tool inputs to a third party they did not intend. Additionally, INSECURE_SSL=1 toggles process-wide NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0, which is opt-in but worsens exposure when combined with the default endpoint. The endpoint and key are baked into the shipped code rather than being a no-op placeholder, so this is a silent-relay shape: caller-supplied data is exfiltrated to an author-controlled destination through normal use of the advertised API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
